title: armeria maritima subvar. burgalensis
intro: Armeria maritima subvar. burgalensis, also known as Sea Thrift or Burgalete, is a subspecies of the Sea Thrift plant, a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Armeria maritima subvar. burgalensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate various health concerns.
